summary.intent: Address 7 Codex review findings on PR #669 (combobox component) and get the PR approved through the repo's required Codex review loop.
summary.outcome: All 7 Codex findings were addressed with regression tests added first, implementations patched, spec updated, and a follow-up commit pushed. The fast pre-push gate passed with 5,953 tests and all 10 CI steps green.

repo: The repo uses a `waiting-for-codex-review` skill that requires posting `@codex review` as a PR comment and then polling reactions/threads: 👀 means in-progress, 👍 means approved, unresolved threads with no 👀 means needs action.
PRs adding `.snap` files require the `snapshot-reviewed` label before Codex review begins.
The fast pre-push gate is `cargo xci --profile fast` and runs 10 steps: fmt, check, clippy, workspace unit tests, integration, adapter, spec snippets, and snapshot-count.
Codex review co-author attribution uses `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>` in commit messages.
`cargo insta test` in this repo does not accept a positional filter argument; the full `--lib` flag without a filter pattern is required for snapshot verification.
The combobox component has iOS-specific behavior gated on an adapter-supplied runtime prop (`is_ios`) affecting active-descendant and VoiceOver handling.
The `cargo xci` tool is the project's unified CI gate with named profiles; `--profile fast` is the pre-push variant.

code.finding: Open-state gating logic: zero-result collections should block open transitions; this was a contract gap found by Codex.
IME (composition) handling: CompositionEnd + Enter key interaction had parity issues with native open behavior.
Filter recompute on item replacement (`update_items`) was not invalidating the filtered view, causing stale results.
Callback-aware blur handling and commit/revert input logic for custom value mode had gaps identified by Codex.
Spec sections for commit/input behavior and adapter-supplied iOS prop surface were updated to reflect new contracts.

workflow: Write regression tests that fail first, then implement — confirmed the red/green cycle before pushing fixes.
Parallel `cargo` invocations contend on the shared build lock; stagger or chain them to avoid silent queuing.
The GraphQL query for fetching PR review threads must use named variables (`$owner`, `$repo`, `$n`) rather than positional; malformed queries silently return empty results from the GitHub API.
Codex review polling requires tracking both reactions API and review thread GraphQL state simultaneously; reactions alone are insufficient to detect 'needs action' state.
Coverage reporting with `cargo llvm-cov` can be separated from test execution by first running `cargo llvm-cov test` and then `cargo llvm-cov report --json` to avoid rerunning tests.

friction: First Codex polling script had a malformed GraphQL query (`}` brace mismatch) causing thread lookups to silently return 0; required a full restart of the polling loop.
The 15-minute polling timeout was hit once due to Codex taking longer than expected, leading to a timeout verdict before Codex actually finished — had to restart polling.
`cargo insta test` rejected positional filter arguments, requiring fallback to full `--lib` snapshot run instead of a focused combobox-only snapshot check.
Multiple cargo commands started in parallel hit the shared Cargo build lock, causing silent queuing and delayed feedback.
Several existing tests had assumptions that conflicted with the stricter zero-result/open semantics introduced by the fix, requiring expectation updates alongside new regression tests.
`cargo fmt --check` found formatting diffs after the implementation patches, requiring a separate `cargo fmt` pass before the pre-push gate.
open_items: Coverage report (`cargo llvm-cov report --show-missing-lines`) was fetched but not fully analyzed — uncovered lines in `combobox/mod.rs` were not enumerated or triaged.
The Codex review loop was not re-triggered after the fix commit was pushed; the transcript ends at the commit without confirming a second `@codex review` trigger or final approval.
iOS active-descendant behavior relies on an adapter-supplied `is_ios` runtime prop — the adapter integration path was not tested end-to-end in this session.
